name: issues_assigned_to_me
description: Fetch issues assigned to the user. Set assigned_to_me=false to broaden the result to all visible issues (so the caller can then filter client-side). Narrow results server-side with repository_name/repository_organization (GitHub), project/issue_type (Jira), or is_closed.

assigned_to_me.description: Whether to limit results to issues assigned to you. Defaults to true.

azure_organization.description: Optionally set the Azure DevOps organization name. Required for Azure DevOps

azure_project.description: Optionally set the Azure DevOps project name. Required for Azure DevOps

fields.description: Optional comma-separated list of top-level fields to keep on each result. Recommended for list/overview calls: omitting it returns every field, which on large lists can overflow the context window. Omit only when you need the full objects. Available fields: id, url, number, provider, repoName, repoOwner, repoHost, project, title, description, issueType, status, assignees, comments, childIssues.

is_closed.description: Set to true to also include closed/done issues. Defaults to false.

issue_type.description: Issue type. Jira only (e.g. Bug, Story, Task).

page.description: Optional parameter to specify the page number, defaults to 1

repository_name.description: Repository name. GitHub only; must be combined with repository_organization.
